single.brief_rationale: The single-agent run successfully completed the full task lifecycle, including EDA, model training with class imbalance handling (threshold tuning), slice metric calculation, and visualization. It correctly identified the worst-performing slice (Male-Rural) and provided concrete mitigation strategies. The provided artifacts (images) confirm high-quality, labeled visualizations. The agent proactively fixed an initial model performance issue (0% recall) without external prompting.

multi.brief_rationale: The multi-agent run exhibited a structured workflow (Planner, Researcher, Reviewer) but suffered from significant technical friction, including import errors, JSON serialization bugs, and an initial model failure (0% recall) that required reviewer intervention. Crucially, the transcript truncates mid-execution in the final step, preventing verification of the final output and mitigation report. While the methodology included validation steps, the execution was unstable and incomplete compared to the single-agent run.

scores.comparison_notes: The single-agent run is the clear winner due to completeness and verifiable success. It produced a full report with high-quality visualizations (confirmed via provided artifacts) and correctly handled the class imbalance issue proactively. The multi-agent run, despite having a more complex workflow structure, failed to complete within the provided transcript (truncation), encountered multiple blocking errors (imports, serialization, model convergence), and relied on reviewer feedback to fix basic model performance issues that the single-agent resolved independently.
